prompt:
Our depot's cold-chain recorder library stopped building the moment we switched it onto the hardened CI profile (optimized + fortified, warnings fatal — the Makefile in the repo is exactly what CI runs). The reader and summary logic have been in the vans all season and the numbers have always matched the paper log, so I don't want any behavior drift out of this — I just need the build green again. Here's what gcc 16 says:

    gcc -std=c17 -O2 -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2 -Wall -Wextra -Wshadow -Werror -c vanlog.c
    vanlog.c:30:20: error: declaration of 't' shadows a previous local [-Werror=shadow]
    vanlog.c:10:5: error: ignoring return value of 'fread' declared with attribute 'warn_unused_result' [-Werror=unused-result]
    vanlog.c:29:19: error: this statement may fall through [-Werror=implicit-fallthrough=]
    vanlog.c:24:12: error: 'lo' may be used uninitialized [-Werror=maybe-uninitialized]
    vanlog.c:24:16: error: 'hi' may be used uninitialized [-Werror=maybe-uninitialized]
    make: *** [Makefile:9: vanlog.o] Error 1

A couple of these look like the compiler being fussy about code that is fine in practice, but the fleet team's rule is that the module has to be genuinely clean, not muted — and where the compiler has a point (that fread, honestly) the failure path should come back as a normal load error, the same -1 the other damaged-file cases return. The trip summaries are pinned by the tests down to the exact counts and temperatures, including what a summary reports when a trip has no temperature-bearing entries at all. Make `make test` pass without touching test_vanlog.c, mintest.h, or the Makefile.
notes: 5W3 item 2: value-safety warnings, all -Werror fatal. Authoring toolchain: gcc 16.1.1; CFLAGS -std=c17 -O2 -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2 -Wall -Wextra -Wshadow -Werror. Probed constraints baked into the flag choice: glibc's fread is warn_unused_result ONLY under fortify (plain -Wall -Wextra never fires -Wunused-result for it), and -Wmaybe-uninitialized needs optimization, hence the realistic hardened profile (-O2 -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2). Planted: (1) fread return ignored on the count-header read — (void) casts do NOT silence gcc's -Wunused-result, and the empty-file test pins the checked path to return -1 like the other damage cases (prompt says so in user voice); (2) maybe-uninitialized lo/hi — set only under a first-flag inside the loop, read unconditionally after; fix initializes to 0.0 and the door-only-trip test pins min=max=0.0; (3) implicit-fallthrough from case 'A' into case 'D' — INTENDED (alerts count as events); tests pin events=2 with one door + one alert so a break-adder fails; reference annotates with __attribute__((fallthrough)) (probed: a /* fall through */ comment with trailing prose does NOT match gcc's level-3 comment regex, so the attribute is the robust fix); (4) -Wshadow: inner `double t` in the 'A' case block shadowing the loop-body t with the same value — fix deletes the inner declaration (behavior identical). Test TU compiles clean under the same flags (fixture fwrite returns are checked — fwrite is also __wur under fortify). Fixtures are written by the harness in-workspace (trip_*.bin, gitignored). Binary round-trip of raw structs is same-ABI same-process, deterministic.
